g.text: RELATIONAL (Postgres, MySQL, Oracle, SQL Server, Spanner, CockroachDB)
GRAPH (Neo4j, JanusGraph, ArangoDB, TigerGraph, AWS Neptune)
TIME-SERIES (InfluxDB, TimescaleDB, Prometheus, VictoriaMetrics, M3, QuestDB)
COLUMNAR / OLAP (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Druid, Pinot, ClickHouse, DuckDB)
VECTOR (Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Milvus, pgvector, Chroma)

text.tspan: Strengths: ACID transactions, complex joins, mature ecosystem, SQL
Use for: orders, payments, accounts, anything with strong consistency needs
Limits: scaling writes hard (sharding adds complexity)
Strengths: O(1) lookup, low latency, simple API, easy to scale horizontally
Use for: caches, session stores, leaderboards, simple lookup tables
Limits: no complex queries, no joins, secondary indexes are afterthoughts
Strengths: flexible schema, nested data, JSON-native, horizontal scale
Use for: catalogs, content management, event payloads, user profiles
Limits: cross-document transactions limited, joins expensive
Strengths: massive write throughput, linear scale, tunable consistency
Use for: time-series, IoT telemetry, event logs, write-heavy workloads
Limits: query patterns must match schema; secondary indexes weak
Strengths: relationship-heavy queries (paths, cycles, communities)
Use for: social networks, recommendations, knowledge graphs, fraud rings
Limits: smaller scale, fewer ops experts, niche use cases
Strengths: full-text, fuzzy, relevance ranking, facets, aggregations
Use for: product search, log search, autocomplete, geospatial
Limits: not a source of truth (eventual consistency); expensive at scale
Strengths: time-partitioned, compression, downsampling, retention policies
Use for: metrics, monitoring, sensor data, financial ticks
Limits: not for general OLTP; queries must be time-bounded
Strengths: massive scans, aggregations, compression, MPP
Use for: BI, analytics, dashboards, ad-hoc data exploration
Limits: not for transactional writes; expensive for small queries
Strengths: cheap, durable, infinite scale, lifecycle policies
Use for: images, videos, logs, backups, data lake, ML datasets
Limits: high per-request latency, no per-byte updates
Strengths: approximate nearest neighbor over embeddings (HNSW, IVF, ScaNN)
Use for: semantic search, RAG, recommendation, anomaly detection
Limits: new tech, ops less mature, often hybrid with inverted index
